diagrams.title: Final Keyword Applications
diagrams.description: Overview showing how final can be applied to variables, methods, and classes with their effects
elements: Variable: [final] type name = value → Cannot reassign
diagrams.purpose: Visual summary of all final keyword applications and outcomes

diagrams.title: Final Variable Types
diagrams.description: Different types of final variables in Java
elements: Instance final: One per object, initialized in constructor
Static final: Class-level, initialized in static block
Local final: Within method, initialized once
Parameter final: Method parameters that cannot be modified
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different scopes and initialization rules for final variables

diagrams.title: Inheritance and Final
elements: Regular class → Can have regular methods (overridable)
Regular class → Can have final methods (not overridable)
Final class → Cannot be subclassed (no children in hierarchy)
Abstract class → Cannot be final (must be extended)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how final restricts inheritance in class diagrams
